name: Cooper Flagg
strengths: Processes the floor instantly, covers space like a wing, rotates early, and gives you real offensive creation without hijacking possessions.
concerns: Handle can still get high in traffic and his half-court pull-up package is more functional than polished right now.
summary: High-floor franchise piece. You draft him to raise the level of every possession on both ends.

name: Ace Bailey
strengths: Length, release point, and scoring confidence are rare. He gets to clean windows other prospects never see.
concerns: Decision-making and shot diet need discipline. Defensive possession-to-possession focus still drifts.
summary: Big swing upside wing. If the process tightens, the ceiling is primary option territory.

name: Kasparas Jakucionis
strengths: Manipulates defenders well, throws every pass, and already understands tempo changes that matter in NBA spacing.
concerns: Finishing craft is ahead of burst. Needs strength to survive against bigger on-ball defenders.
summary: Strong lead-guard bet if you value processing and shot versatility over raw explosion.

name: Tre Johnson
strengths: One of the cleanest perimeter scorers in the class. Comfortable hitting from movement, relocation, and quick lift situations.
concerns: Creation pressure at the rim is still limited. Defensive strength and screen navigation need work.
summary: Premium scoring specialist with enough polish to play early.

name: Derrion Reid
strengths: Strong body, clean passing reads, and a game that scales without demanding touches.
concerns: The jumper still decides the outcome. Without enough shooting growth, the offensive value tightens.
summary: Reliable team concept forward if the shot gets to credible volume efficiency.

name: Egor Demin
strengths: Sees over the defense, keeps the ball moving, and creates passing angles that smaller guards simply do not have.
concerns: The jumper is the swing skill and defenders will test it immediately. Needs more downhill force.
summary: Bet on uncommon playmaking dimensions and pray the shot stabilizes.

name: Liam McNeeley
strengths: Quick decisions, real shooting touch, and smart secondary reads make him easy to fit next to stronger creators.
concerns: Average burst limits some self-creation. Defensive versatility is more scheme-based than disruptive.
summary: High-utility wing for teams that need spacing and connective offense.

name: Asa Newell
strengths: Runs hard, plays above the floor, and gives you backline activity with real energy every night.
concerns: Shot and half-court creation profile remain unproven. Needs a clearer defensive matchup identity.
summary: Useful modern frontcourt athlete if you are comfortable developing the offensive skill package.

name: Drake Powell
strengths: Takes hard assignments, moves his feet, and creates pressure with length and balance on the ball.
concerns: Offensive self-creation is still light. Needs more certainty as a shooter to lock in a clean role.
summary: Wing defender archetype that becomes much more valuable if the jumper becomes real.

name: Kam Jones
strengths: Craft, pull-up confidence, and offensive maturity should translate quickly into bench scoring value.
concerns: Older profile with less physical upside. Defensive size and rim pressure cap the archetype.
summary: Second-unit scoring guard who can outperform slot if the shot versatility holds up.

name: Hunter Sallis
strengths: Comfortable creating from the elbows and can score in broken-floor possessions when structure dies.
concerns: Needs to prove enough shooting efficiency and defensive buy-in to avoid becoming a tweener score-first bet.
summary: Interesting value swing for teams that need extra creation in the second round.

name: Boogie Fland
strengths: Creates separation, changes pace, and already understands how to play off screens with scoring intent.
concerns: Size and strength will define how much of the on-ball diet survives against NBA defenders.
summary: Smaller creator bet with real shotmaking upside if the physical threshold is manageable.
